macro_summary: Monday's session saw tech selloff deepen — SPY -0.78%, QQQ -1.08% — as oil surged to $83.63 (+2.26%) on continued US-Iran strikes and Hormuz tensions, pushing VIX to 18.77 (+12.3%). The June CPI cooled to 3.5% but the oil spike threatens to reignite inflation, keeping the Fed on hold before the July 29 decision. TSMC raised its capex to $60-64B after a 77% profit jump, but semis sold off anyway as the rotation from high-flyers persisted. This week's Google, Intel, and Nokia earnings will test the AI capex thesis.
commodities_note: WTI $83.63, up 17% in 5 days, as the Hormuz crisis enters its 8th week with the US conducting a 7th night of strikes. Gold slipped 1.8% over the week to $4,029. DXY flat at 100.72. Oil's spike threatens to unwind the CPI cool; ECB rethinking rate path.

singapore.summary: All three Singapore banks are strongly extended — DBS and OCBC hard-blocked on +11% and +16% above SMA50, while UOB lags — and NIM compression from high US rates and the strong SGD flat out refuses to ease. The next MAS decision on 31 July is the immediate watch; until then, no entry exists.
singapore.key_development: OCBC maintained its relative outperformance, but R:R 0.4:1 with RSI 83 makes a pullback the only realistic entry path.

semiconductors.summary: Broad selling continues — SOXX -0.7% to 521.81 and -10.2% in five days — with NVDA and AMD both below their 50‑day average. TSMC raised capex to $60‑64B but the stock sold off anyway, underscoring a rotation out of the AI‑capex complex. This week’s Google, Intel, and Nokia prints are the immediate regime marker for capex sustainability — if any major guide disappoints, the whole cluster faces a thesis‑break check.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Structurally bullish, tactically broken — earnings tests ahead
semiconductors.key_development: Google’s capex guidance on 22 July is the single most important cluster catalyst; a cut would cascade across the semis/memory/optics complex.

big_tech.summary: META is hard‑blocked on extension with a declining 50‑day average, while AMZN and MSFT hover near their SMAs. Google reports in two days — its capex call will not only drive BigTech but also ripple through the entire AI‑supply chain. PLTR remains expensive and mechanically blocked. With three of five names holding earnings within ten days, the cluster is one big binary bet this week.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — capex confirmed but monetization questioned
big_tech.key_development: Google’s 22 July earnings are the lead domino; a capex guide‑down would rewrite the cluster’s structural conviction.

interconnected.name: Coherent
interconnected.reason: NVDA $2B photonics investment and InP capacity doubling directly benefit the AI‑optical buildout alongside LITE — Coherent is the other leg of NVIDIA's photonics bet.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if Coherent tests its 50‑day SMA on a post‑earnings semis reset, and only after verifying that its 6‑inch InP yields are improving (per the IA optic‑supply‑chain deep‑dive).

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC and optical DSP beneficiary — hyperscaler capex flows directly into data‑center networking silicon, making MRVL a high‑beta read on the same capex line as NVDA/AVGO.
interconnected.entry_note: Track after GOOG and MSFT capex guidance; becomes interesting on a pullback to its 200‑day average only if AI‑networking revenue growth accelerates above 30%.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: Data‑center switching leader tied to hyperscaler AI buildout — each new GPU cluster drives 400G/800G switch upgrades, making Arista a levered play on capex deployment speed.
interconnected.entry_note: Revisit after Arista earnings on 30 July for AI‑networking revenue acceleration; add to watchlist if the print confirms 40%+ cloud‑titan growth and the stock pulls back to its 50‑day average.

active_risks: US‑Iran Hormuz conflict: 7th night of CENTCOM airstrikes, oil above $83, Strait of Hormuz shipping severely disrupted; no diplomatic off‑ramp visible.
Trump administration racing to rebuild the tariff wall — 25% duties on Brazil under Section 301, with China export‑control and helium‑ban risks still live.
Oil spike threatens to unwind the CPI cool; ECB rethinking its rate path, Fed holding ahead of the 29 July FOMC.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil elevated is compressing tech multiples — SOXX -10% in five days, BigTech earnings binary looming — while Singapore banks remain extended despite the regional safe‑haven bid. Semis are caught between structural demand (TSMC capex raise) and rotation fear; the Google print tomorrow is the line in the sand.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Oil rose 2.3% to $83.63 as US strikes continued; a reported attack killed three US servicemembers, escalating the conflict. Google earnings tomorrow are now the most consequential catalyst for the AI‑capex complex.

base.description: Hormuz stalemate holds, WTI $80‑85; semis remain under pressure but Google and Microsoft capex guidance provides a floor, preventing a full‑on capitulation. Big‑3 Singapore banks stay extended, no entry.

optimistic.description: A diplomatic breakthrough or ceasefire eases oil below $75; the tech rotation reverses as inflation fears recede, and the semis cluster rallies off support, with NVDA and AMD leading a sharp bounce.

pessimistic.description: Iran escalates — coordinated tanker targeting pushes oil toward $100; VIX spikes above 25, broad selloff accelerates, and semis break below their 200‑day averages, forcing a cluster‑wide thesis‑break check.

wildcard.description: China enters the conflict or bans more materials; the helium export halt becomes acute, triggering a supply‑chain shock that compounds the oil crisis and hits ASML, IFX, and NVTS far harder than geopolitics alone.

geopolitical.portfolio_action: Deploy no new capital this week; wait for GOOG/INTC earnings and the FOMC on 29 July to reset risk‑reward before adding any exposure.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 3 of 32 names are hard‑blocked on technical extension; the deepening semis selloff and the oil spike threaten to unwind the inflation cooldown. Google’s capex guidance tomorrow is the first true regime marker for the entire AI‑supply‑chain watchlist.
